subject: Cloud Computing
title: Message Queues and Decoupling
description: A fast breakdown of Message Queues and Decoupling for builders.

hook.text: A user uploads a video. Processing takes 10 minutes. Do they wait? No — a message queue handles it in the background.

buildup.text: Message queues let services communicate asynchronously. The sender drops a message; the receiver picks it up later.

discovery.text: AWS SQS, RabbitMQ, and Apache Kafka are popular choices. Each handles different scales and patterns.

twist.text: Queues add latency by design. Data isn't processed instantly — it waits in line. That delay must be acceptable.

climax.text: Queues absorb traffic spikes like a buffer. Producers can burst while consumers process at their own pace.

punchline.text: Queues absorb the burst. Consumers work at their own speed.

quiz.question: What is the primary benefit of using message queues?
options: Services communicate asynchronously, absorbing traffic spikes
Messages are processed instantly with zero latency
Queues eliminate the need for databases entirely
